data.title: Nerdy Inc. reports strong Q2 2025 results, revenue growth and beat EBITDA guidance, aiming for profitability in Q4.
data.description: Nerdy Inc. (NYSE: NRDY) revealed its financial performance for the second quarter ended June 30, 2025, in St. Louis. Chuck Cohn, the Founder, Chairman, and CEO of Nerdy, stated that the Live+AI™ strategy was instrumental in driving growth in Q2. The company witnessed a resurgence in Learning Membership revenue and exceeded EBITDA expectations. Additionally, the introduction of AI-native tools aimed at empowering learners and educators with real-time capabilities is underway. These developments have positioned Nerdy on a path towards profitability by Q4. For more information, kindly refer to the Nerdy invest.

data.title: Consensus Cloud Solutions, Inc. (NASDAQ: CCSI) reported strong Q2 results, with 6.9% growth in corporate revenue. They reaffirmed full-year revenue guidance and raised adjusted earnings per share.
data.description: In Los Angeles, Consensus Cloud Solutions, Inc. (NASDAQ: CCSI) released its financial report for the second quarter of 2025. The company showcased positive revenue growth surpassing expectations, with Corporate revenue up by 6.9% compared to the same period last year. This growth was fueled by increased usage, enhanced revenue retention, and successful customer acquisition efforts. SoHo revenue met projections and operating margins stayed strong, indicating a healthy financial performance for the quarter.

data.title: VTEX reports Q2 2025 results, following U.S. GAAP & SEC regulations. Geraldo Thomaz Jr. shares positive investment.
data.description: In New York, VTEX (NYSE: VTEX) reported its second-quarter results for 2025 ending on June 30. The financial report adheres to U.S. GAAP and SEC regulations. VTEX, known as the foundation for connected commerce, saw positive developments. Geraldo Thomaz Jr., the co-CEO and founder of VTEX, expressed satisfaction with ongoing investments and growth strategies.

data.title: The Trade Desk reports strong Q2 results with revenue up 19% to $694 million, exceeding digital advertising market growth.
data.description: In Los Angeles, The Trade Desk, Inc. (NASDAQ: TTD) revealed its second-quarter financial results for the period ending June 30, 2025. The company, a leading provider of a global advertising technology platform, reported a revenue increase to $694 million, representing a 19% growth compared to the previous year. CEO and Co-Founder, Jeff Green, highlighted the strong performance of the second quarter, emphasizing the company's continuous outpacing of the digital advertising market.

data.title: Expedia Group's Q2 2025 results: Booked room nights up 7%, total gross bookings up 5%, revenue up 6%. B2B and Advertising led growth.
data.description: Expedia Group, Inc. shared its financial results for the quarter ending June 30, 2025. Key highlights include a 7% increase in booked room nights, particularly fueled by international growth. Total gross bookings saw a 5% rise, with a significant 17% surge in B2B gross bookings; B2C gross bookings went up by 1%. Lodging gross bookings increased by 6%, with hotel bookings rising by 8% due to B2B and Brand Expedia. The revenue also showed growth at 6%, primarily driven by a 15% increase in B2B and Advertising sectors.

data.title: Megaport reaches 1,000 enabled locations globally, offering access in 10% of public data centers. World's leading NaaS provider.
data.description: In Brisbane, Australia, Megaport Limited (ASX: MP1), the top global Network as a Service provider, has revealed the launch of its 1,000th Megaport-enabled location. This achievement means Megaport now offers connectivity from 10% of all public data centers globally, solidifying its position as the leading vendor-neutral NaaS connectivity platform worldwide. Formerly a disruptor in the connectivity sector, Megaport has evolved into the preferred solution for IT teams needing reliable and flexible cloud networking services.

data.title: Expensify (Nasdaq: EXFY) reports Q2 2025 results, with branding shown over 650 times in F1® The Movie.
data.description: Expensify, Inc. (Nasdaq: EXFY) based in Portland, Oregon, has recently shared its quarterly results for the quarter ending June 30, 2025. The company, known for its payments superapp facilitating money management for individuals and businesses globally, published a shareholder letter from Founder and CEO David Barrett. Q2 demonstrated significant growth, especially with F1® The Movie featuring the Expensify brand in over 650 instances on screen. This exposure has had a substantial impact on the company's visibility and brand recognition.

data.title: Corsair Gaming's Q2 2025 shows impressive results: double-digit year-over-year growth in revenue and gross profit. #Corsair #Q22025
data.description: A company based in Milpitas, California, Corsair Gaming, Inc. (Nasdaq: CRSR), a prominent global provider of high-performance products for gamers, streamers, content creators, gaming PC builders, and SIM driving enthusiasts, has released its financial outcomes for the second quarter concluding on June 30, 2025. Corsair has reported a robust quarter marked by double-digit revenue and gross profit growth, increased margins, and widespread demand vitality across its various product categories.

data.title: The Trade Desk appoints Omar Tawakol to its board, bringing over 20 years of experience in ad tech and AI. CEO Jeff Green excited about the addition.
data.description: In Ventura, California, The Trade Desk (Nasdaq: TTD) declared the addition of tech veteran Omar Tawakol to its board of directors. Tawakol, boasting over 20 years in advertising technology, data platforms, and artificial intelligence, joins the company. The CEO and Co-founder, Jeff Green, praised Tawakol’s expertise, labeling him as a leading figure in ad tech and AI.

data.title: Ross Stores, Inc. (Nasdaq: ROST) to release Q2 earnings on Aug 21, 2025, at 4:00 pm ET, with a conference call at 4:15 pm ET. Website for live audio webcast.
data.description: Dublin, California - Ross Stores, Inc. (Nasdaq: ROST) is set to announce its second quarter 2025 earnings on August 21, 2025, at around 4:00 p.m. EST. A live audio webcast of the conference call will be accessible on the Company’s website, www.rossstores.com, on the same day at 4:15 p.m. EST. For those unable to tune in at that time, a recorded version of the call will also be available on the website. Stay tuned for the financial updates from Ross Stores, Inc.

data.title: LegalZoom announces strong Q2 2025 results, raises revenue growth expectations for 2025.
data.description: Mountain View, California - On August 7, 2025, LegalZoom.com, Inc. revealed its financial performance for the second quarter concluding on June 30, 2025. The company's stock is listed on Nasdaq under the symbol LZ.

data.title: Key Tronic Corp. (Nasdaq: KTCC) to report Q4 and FY results for fiscal 2025 on August 27, 2025 after market close.
data.description: Key Tronic Corporation, based in Spokane Valley, Washington, revealed on Aug. 7, 2025, that it intends to disclose its financial outcomes for the fourth quarter and full year of fiscal 2025 subsequent to the close of the market on August 27, 2025. The company, listed as Nasdaq: KTCC, will provide details about its performance during this period.

data.title: ePlus (NASDAQ: PLUS) declares first quarterly cash dividend of $0.25 per share, to be paid in Sept.
data.description: On August 7, 2025, ePlus inc., a prominent technology solutions provider based in Herndon, Virginia, declared its inaugural quarterly dividend. The Board of Directors of ePlus inc. announced the decision to pay a quarterly cash dividend of $0.25 per common share. Shareholders can expect to receive this dividend on September...

data.title: Marpai will host webcast on Aug 14, 2025 to discuss Q2 2025 financial results. Conference call at 8:30am ET in Tampa.
data.description: In Tampa, Florida on August 7, 2025, Marpai, Inc. (OTCQX: MRAI), a prominent player in cutting-edge healthcare technology and Third-Party Administration (TPA) services, is set to conduct a conference call and webcast on Thursday, August 14 at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time.

data.title: BioLife Solutions had a successful Q2 2025 with $23.0 million in Cell Processing revenue, 62% GAAP gross margin, and $15.8 million GAAP net loss.
data.description: Revenue from Cell Processing saw a significant increase, reaching $23.0 million, a 28% rise from Q2 2024. The GAAP gross margin stood at 62%, while the non-GAAP adjusted gross margin was reported at 65%. Despite this positive performance, there was a GAAP net loss of $15.8 million, which included a $15.5 million non-cash IPR&D expense. On the non-GAAP side, the adjusted EBITDA was $6.1 million, representing 24% of the revenue. These numbers indicate a promising outlook for the company, showing significant growth potential in the market.
